SUBJECTIVE
Primary Diagnosis:
Encounter Diagnosis:
#1 Weakness (R531)
#1 Weakness (R531)
#2 Dizziness and giddiness (R42)
#2 Dizziness and giddiness (R42)
Chief Complaint: Akeia presents today with reports of work related injury January 4, 2026. She reports while working at Target
warehouse a tote fell from a high shelf onto her head. She denies loss of consciousness. She reports she was evaluated at
emergency receiving a CT scan that she reports cleared her but notes concussion and post-concussion syndrome. Akeia reports
difficulty with daily tasks, weakness, sensitivity to light, headaches, neck pain, neck tightness, dizziness, and difficulty with
balance. She rates pain at a high of 7/10 and at best 7/10. Akeia reports she recently returned to work in October 2025 from a
previous work related injury (slip and fall) in June 2025.
Current Functional Status: See Dizziness Handicap Inventory questionnaire
Patient function prior to injury: In the past, the patient reported independence in the following areas: ADLs, Work
Productivity, Ambulation, Community Involvement, Independent Exercise, Miscellaneous, Reports she previously could complete
10 hour shifts for work at Target warehouse, a couple days a week at a school working with developmentally disabled children,
caring for home and husband and children (8, 9, 21, 24 yo).
Current Functional Limitations: Currently, the patient reports limitations in the following areas: Care Of Self,
Sitting/Standing, ADLs, Sleeping, Squatting, Pulling/Pushing, Community Involvement, Miscellaneous
Functional deficit affecting: No
Pain Scale: Worst: 7 Current: 7
Current Medications: See intake forms
OBJECTIVE
OUTCOME SCORE
1# Dizziness Handicap Inventory test (Advanced) - 88
2# Dizziness Handicap Inventory test (Advanced) - 96
OBSERVATION
OBSERVATION Able to ambulate from waiting room to treatment room. Though is slow cautious and with a wide base of
support.
ROM/MANUAL MUSCLE TESTS
RANGE OF MOTION/MANUAL MUSCLE TESTS Cervical spine range of motion
Flexion 1 finger from chest
